verses.text: God bids them search Jerusalem for one who does justice and seeks truth, that He might pardon it. Honest men are scarce. A single righteous soul could have stayed judgment, but they could not be found.

God bids them search Jerusalem for one who does justice and seeks truth, that He might pardon it. Honest men are scarce. A single righteous soul could have stayed judgment, but they could not be found.

God bids them search Jerusalem for one who does justice and seeks truth, that He might pardon it. Honest men are scarce. A single righteous soul could have stayed judgment, but they could not be found.

Jeremiah excused the poor as ignorant, but found even the great had broken God's yoke; so beasts of prey shall tear them. Sin runs from low to high. The educated and powerful proved as faithless as the simple.

Jeremiah excused the poor as ignorant, but found even the great had broken God's yoke; so beasts of prey shall tear them. Sin runs from low to high. The educated and powerful proved as faithless as the simple.

Jeremiah excused the poor as ignorant, but found even the great had broken God's yoke; so beasts of prey shall tear them. Sin runs from low to high. The educated and powerful proved as faithless as the simple.

'How shall I pardon thee?' God asks, for they swore by no-gods and played the adulterer; 'shall I not visit for these things?' Their sin demands reckoning. Persistent, unrepented evil cannot be left unpunished.

'How shall I pardon thee?' God asks, for they swore by no-gods and played the adulterer; 'shall I not visit for these things?' Their sin demands reckoning. Persistent, unrepented evil cannot be left unpunished.

'How shall I pardon thee?' God asks, for they swore by no-gods and played the adulterer; 'shall I not visit for these things?' Their sin demands reckoning. Persistent, unrepented evil cannot be left unpunished.
He sends the foe to destroy her vine-rows, for they denied Him and said the prophets were 'wind.' They mocked His word. Those who scorn God's warnings prove them true by their ruin.
He sends the foe to destroy her vine-rows, for they denied Him and said the prophets were 'wind.' They mocked His word. Those who scorn God's warnings prove them true by their ruin.

He sends the foe to destroy her vine-rows, for they denied Him and said the prophets were 'wind.' They mocked His word. Those who scorn God's warnings prove them true by their ruin.

God will make His words in Jeremiah's mouth a fire, and bring 'a nation from far' to devour their harvests and children. The word becomes judgment. The despised prophecy is fulfilled by an invading power.

God will make His words in Jeremiah's mouth a fire, and bring 'a nation from far' to devour their harvests and children. The word becomes judgment. The despised prophecy is fulfilled by an invading power.

God will make His words in Jeremiah's mouth a fire, and bring 'a nation from far' to devour their harvests and children. The word becomes judgment. The despised prophecy is fulfilled by an invading power.

'Nevertheless... I will not make a full end'; because they served strange gods, they shall serve strangers in a foreign land. The punishment fits the crime. Those who chose other gods are given to other masters.

'Nevertheless... I will not make a full end'; because they served strange gods, they shall serve strangers in a foreign land. The punishment fits the crime. Those who chose other gods are given to other masters.

Fearless of the God who set the sand as the sea's bound, they no longer say, 'Let us now fear the LORD,' who gives the rain. They ignore His daily mercies. Forgetting God's faithful providence is the root of their irreverence.

Fearless of the God who set the sand as the sea's bound, they no longer say, 'Let us now fear the LORD,' who gives the rain. They ignore His daily mercies. Forgetting God's faithful providence is the root of their irreverence.

Their sins withhold good from them; among them are men who set traps and grow rich by deceit; 'shall I not visit for these things?' Injustice fills the land. God will reckon with a people who prosper by oppression.

Their sins withhold good from them; among them are men who set traps and grow rich by deceit; 'shall I not visit for these things?' Injustice fills the land. God will reckon with a people who prosper by oppression.

'A wonderful and horrible thing': prophets prophesy falsely, priests rule by their means, 'and my people love to have it so.' The deceit is welcomed. The saddest ruin is a people content to be told lies.
